titlestmt.titleproper: David Lehman's Alternative Community School records6901

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-04 00:16:47 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: Undetermined, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: David Lehman's Alternative Community School records

odd.head: General Note
odd.p: On deposit from the Ithaca City School District.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The records of the Lehman Alternative Community School in Ithaca, NY, which include administrative records, teaching materials, yearbooks, newsletters, flags, pictures, and other files. Also present are many publications and other materials on alternative education.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David Lehman's Alternative Community School Records, #6901. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Dave Lehman founded the Alternative Community School in 1974, and served as its principal until he retired in 2004. The combined middle school and high school is a public school, part of the Ithaca City School District, that operates based on alternatives to traditional curriculum and school governance philosophies. After his retirement, the school was renamed the Lehman Alternative Community School.

did.unittitle: ACS dessert and discussion night notes
ACS Staff Sabbatic proposals and reports
Alternative Community School. Handbook of Classroom Strategies for Students With Special Needs
Alternative School Faces Budget Crisis-- Jennifer Sprague, Ithaca New Times
Archaeologists of the Soul: a Collection of Stories and Projects
Brief Look at Public Alternative Schools in Ontario
Creative Writing Contest Winning Entries, Tompkins-Seneca-Tioga BOCES
Guidebook to Learning Experiences in the Alternative Program, State College PA 1980-81
LEAD -- Leadership Education and Development teaching guide
National Coalition News, a publication of National Coalition of Alternative Community Schools
New Schools Exchange Newsletter, no. 134. Education for Change Conference Report

scopecontent.p: Includes copies of the Wineskin, the Wash, A Student's Guide to ACS Brand Democracy, the Ox's Sox , Youth Speak, and Parallax.

Vox of the Ox, ACS newsletter
What the Media Covered about the War in the Persian Gulf: a media study by high school students, the Alternative Community School, Ithaca, NY

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sub-series includes the records kept by Beverly Reed, LACS librarian. However, constituting the bulk of the sub-series are the school archives, which Reed had been entrusted with, and continued to be added to after her tenure. Among the records present are school publications, newspaper clippings, meeting materials, and Ithaca City School District records.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Possessing no discernable original order, sub-series has been intellectually arranged in alphabetical order.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sub-series comprised chiefly of teaching materials kept by Karen Adams, whose courses included Latin American Studies, U.S. History, and Constitutional Law. She also coached the mock trial team.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Possessing no discernable original order, sub-series has been intellectually arranged in alphabetical order.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Adams was a Social Studies teacher and also served on the LACS Archives Committee.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Diane Carruthers served as Associate Principal from 2011 until 2012, and Principal from 2013 until 2018.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly materials for the Social Studies and English courses Colman taught, which included Western Civilization and Creative Writing. Also included are a folder of materials for the Cornell Freshman English course Colman taught in the 1960s.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Records found loosely based on subject matter.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Colman and her husband Gould, were active participants in the creation of LACS in 1974. During her 19 year tenure, Colman taught social studies and English courses.

King Arthur. The Acts of Arthur, John Steinbeck
The art of the novel and novella
Elements of culture: worksheets and readings
Neolithic revolution - domestication of plants and animals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sub-series contains course materials from Flerlage's Algebra, Physics, Trigonometry and Ecology courses.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged in numbered binders by course.

did.unittitle: Algebra, Physics, Trigonometry class description and box contents
Binder #1. Course description, individual and small group final narrative evaluation, examples of working daily plans and reflections

Binder #2. Course introduction, course expectations & structure, seeing mathematical relationships in physical contexts, basic algebra review & practice, "Thinking Physics", constant velocity & graphical form, introducing simultaneous equations to predict intersections, distance as area under the curve
Binder #3. Introducing acceleration; Falling bodies, physically & graphically; Moving between tables, graphs, and equations; Where are compenents in a graph?, Introducing the quadratic equation
Binder #4. Forces causing motion; Forces at angles; Force components as an intro to trigonometry; Friction's impact on objects in motion
Binder #5. Putting trig to work; Friction on an inclined plane; Right triangle trig: sine, cosine; tangent; Periodic motion; the ferris wheel problem
Binder #6. Derivation of the quadratic formula through physics
Binder #7. Projectile motion unit; Kinetic & potential energy; Collision, conservation of momentum, and accident reconstruction unit
Ecology class description and box contents
Folder #1. Course description; Coure expectations; Course portfolio cover sheets; Assorted student reflections
Folder #1B. Course expections; How to successfully complete this course; Note to parent/caregivers
Folder #2. Articles illustrating the idea that everything is connected; Terrestial ecosystem study; Final assessment of nutrient cycles unit
Folder #2B. Portfolio contents/guide; Portfolio organizational notes; Portfolio coversheet
Folder #3B. Examples of each 3 components of course final assessments; Course essay component; Final student lab design
Folder #4. Sense of place: student reflections
Folder #4B. A small sample of unit level labs; Concept mapping and readings
Folder #5. Excerpts from unit critiquing current human designed food systems; Use of concept maps as a means of graphically/pictorially representing interconnections/positive feedback loops
Folder #5B. Materials associated with our evolution unit lab -- "Using Protein Gel Electrophoresis to Study Evolution"
Folder #6. Excerpts of energy system, critique/climate change unit
Folder #6B. Materials associated with one of our DNA labs: "DNA Profiling in the Courtroom: Using DNA Restrictions and Comparison and Gets to Solve a Mock Murder"
Folder #7. A selection of articles used during the year offering perspectives on the concept of sustainable human system design
Folder #7B. Materials associated with the PCR lab: forensic DNA amplification. A lab made possible through the help of Cornell staff and graduate students
Folder #8B. Materials associated with our lab: "Investigating the Process of Mutation"
Folder #9B. Materials associated with the Wolbachia lab, part of the Wolbachia project: searching for the presence of Wolbachia in local insects
Proposal to change ACS decision making process (complete set of documents)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sub-series includes LACS records on teaching, student productions, and personal creative writing. Some materials date back to Goldsmith's tenure at Boynton Junior High School. Also included are Goldsmith's records created while anb instructor/tutor at the Lansing Residential Center after her retirement from LACS in 2004.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Sub-series found organized into two distinct sub-series: Goldsmith's LACS records and Lansing Residential Center records. The former includes a subcategory on Shakespeare. Lacking original order, files have been intellectually arranged in alphabetical order.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Goldsmith taught English and theatre, directed school plays, and chaperoned students during ACS Trips Week. After her retirement in 2004 she taught and tutored students at the Lansing Residential Center.

Staff workshops-- 11/7/79 - learning styles, Lynn, Dave, contracts- art; 1/80 stress reduction
Student progress reports and staffing forms

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Records stored by David Lehman, which include administrative records, publications, realia, photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Lacking in original order, this sub-series has been intellectually arranged in alphabetical order.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Lehman was the founding principal of LACS, having been installed at the newly created New Junior High Program (NJHP) in 1974. He oversaw its growth as it incorporated new grades, eventually becoming the Alternative Community School (ACS) in 1981. The school was renamed the Lehman Alternative Community School (LACS) following his and his wife's retirement in 2004 (Judy Lehman served as school secretary since the school's founding).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Material include blank school forms and information sheets.

did.unittitle: ACS handbook -- classroom strategies -- children with special needs
ACS shared decision making model/accountability system
ACS Superintendent's Conference Day feedback forms
Alternative schools, ACS courses and requirements
Annual Report for 1993-94 to NYSED from ACS in Ithaca on "Graduation By Exhibition" variances -- an assessment of progress toward learner outcome prepared by Dave Lehman, principal
Book of Fantasy and Adventures by 100 Students From Alternative Community Schools
Course booklets and high school graduation requirements
Course expectation sheets: Computer II E.
Course expectation sheets: I. projects + sr. seminar + trips + community studies
Course expectation sheets: II A. read write listen speak English
Course expectation sheets: II B. languages other
Course expectation sheets: II C. math
Course expectation sheets: II D. & designers and performers -- IV
First Year Evaluation Report New Junior High Program 1974-75

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Floppy disks contain course materials, correspondence, and staff lists.

ICSD -- student rights and responsibilities handbook

Introduction of Group Process Concepts to Maximize Student and Teacher Learning in an Alternative Junior High School, Karen Adams, Masters Thesis
Ithaca bike history posters/Ithaca bike plan map
Lawsuit -- NY Performance Standards Consortium vs. NY State Ed. Dept. and Richard Mills, vs. Commissioner of Ed. In State of NY (court documents)
Manual Theatre Art 212: Basics of Acting

scopecontent.p: Materials include articles, newsletters, group activity sheets, and culinary arts program descriptions.

scopecontent.p: Materials include newsletters, flyers, and event programs.

scopecontent.p: Tapes chiefly unlabelled. Include performances and school activities.
